summary: SE Asia PP yarn/inj fell 50 USD to 1,045 USD/ton CFR (Platts)
China FOB at 1,065 USD/ton limits export competitiveness
NSRP 114G cut to 30,500 VND/kg (down 500); 7,000-ton tender opened
paras: According to Platts pricing, in Northeast Asia the price level of PP yarn/injection and IPP Film this week fell only slightly, by 5 USD/ton compared to the previous week, while BOPP fell 15 USD/ton. In Southeast Asia, the price level of PP yarn/injection fell 50 USD/ton compared to the previous week, down to 1,045 USD/ton/CFR; BOPP fell 50 USD/ton, down to 1,065 USD/ton/CFR; IPP Film fell 50 USD/ton, down to 1,095 USD/ton/CFR; while PP block copolymer fell sharply by 100 USD/ton, down to 1,045 USD/ton/CFR, on par with the PP homo price.

Thus, PP prices in Asia continued to fall sharply this week, driven by falling prices of the input material propylene. PP export offers from China were quite limited, given the FOB China price of 1,065 USD/ton, while price levels in the main export markets such as Southeast Asia are currently lower than domestic Chinese PP prices.

In Vietnam, the leading PP factories are still operating at maximum capacity. The NSRP plant just opened a tender to sell 7,000 tons of PP, with delivery from July 7–20, 2026. The quantity may increase or decrease within a 20% range depending on NSRP’s choice.

In domestic transactions, a large distributor of NSRP lowered the offer price of PP 114G to 30,500 VND/kg with VAT, with delivery in the north, down 500 VND/kg compared to the start of the week. Some Chinese-origin PP yarn grades were offered down to 30,000 VND/kg with VAT.

“Many commercial sellers are still trying to keep PP offers high, with PP homo offered in the range of 30,500–32,000 VND/kg with VAT; but in reality, in the north, the common transaction level for PP yarn is currently only just above or below 30,000 VND/kg with VAT,” a northern source said.

In the south, PP T3034 of BSR and PP yarn of Hyosung Vina are commonly offered at 31,500–31,800 VND/kg with VAT, but without achieving many transactions. Many buyers believe this price level is still higher than the general level and are choosing to stay out of the market to wait for prices to fall further.

summary: NE Asia HDPE fell 25 USD; SE Asia HDPE down 30 to 1,150-1,160 CFR
Qamar settles LLDPE 860-870, HDPE Film F1 960 USD/ton CIF
Domestic LDPE Film down to 39,000-41,000 VND/kg (down 1-4k/kg)
paras: According to Platts pricing published today, July 2, in Northeast Asia, the price level of HDPE Film/yarn/injection/blow fell 25 USD/ton compared to the previous week, and LLDPE fell 25 USD/ton, while LDPE rose slightly by 5 USD/ton after its sharp drop the previous week. In Southeast Asia, the price level of HDPE grades this week fell 30 USD/ton, down to 1,150–1,160 USD/ton/CFR; LLDPE fell 20 USD/ton, down to 1,150 USD/ton/CFR; LLDPE metallocene C6 fell 20 USD/ton, down to 1,300 USD/ton/CFR; conversely, LDPE rose 30 USD/ton compared to the previous week, up to 1,450 USD/ton/CFR...

In general, the downward price trend in the Asian PE market continued this week, driven by falling input material prices and weak demand restraining price negotiations. In Southeast Asia and Vietnam, very few transactions took place, as many buyers maintained a cautious stance amid the weakening across the entire PE supply chain.

Today, the PE selling price from Qamar (Saudi Arabia) for the July–August 2026 shipment lots was announced by distributor Mitsubishi to Vietnamese buyers. The settled price level somewhat “shocked” the market. Specifically, LLDPE FD21HN/FC21HN/FD18N was settled at 860 USD/ton/CIF and LLDPE FD21HS at 870 USD/ton/CIF, lower than the offer by up to 90 USD/ton. At the same time, HDPE Film F1 was settled at 960 USD/ton/CIF, lower than the offer by 70 USD/ton. CIF Da Nang goods carry an additional 20 USD/ton.
The distributor of Lotte Chemical Korea also announced new settled selling prices for some LLDPE Film/injection lots delivered in August 2026 for buyers in Vietnam.

Accordingly, the settled selling price of Lotte’s LLDPE injection UL814 was 1,010 USD/ton/CIF, 20 USD/ton lower than the offer price at the start of the week. The settled price of Lotte’s LLDPE Film UF414 for customers buying under 200 tons was 980 USD/ton/CIF, also 20 USD/ton lower than the offer price at the start of the week. However, for Film goods, Lotte offered various incentives based on quantity: customers buying 200–300 tons received a settled price of 970 USD/ton/CIF; 300–400 tons, 960 USD/ton/CIF; and 400 tons and above, 950 USD/ton/CIF.

Previously, yesterday, the distributor of Sabic (Saudi Arabia) quoted settled HDPE selling prices for July to Vietnamese buyers, CIF from Singapore: settled HDPE Film FI0750 CIF from Saudi Arabia at 1,120 USD/ton/CIF; the same code but CIF from Singapore was quoted at 1,160 USD/ton/CIF (if customers buy 400 tons and above, the settled price is 1,140 USD/ton/CIF). The settled price of HDPE FI8051 and FJ00952 was 1,100–1,120 USD/ton/CIF (customers buying 500 tons and above receive a settled price 20 USD/ton lower); settled HDPE injection M200056 at 1,270 USD/ton/CIF and HDPE injection M80063S at 1,180 USD/ton/CIF...

In addition, one distributor offered LDPE Film 4025AS/1925AS of Tasnee (Saudi Arabia) to Vietnam at 1,250 USD/ton/CIF. This is 50 USD/ton lower than the LDPE Film selling price Sabic announced late last week (1,300 USD/ton/CIF). Another distributor settled LDPE Film of Qapco (Qatar) for Vietnamese buyers at a notably lower level of 1,180 USD/ton/CIF — the Middle Eastern-origin LDPE Film with the lowest price at this time...

Domestically, after the fairly sharp drop at the start of the week, by mid-week the number of sellers adjusting prices was more limited, as they waited for customer feedback on the new price level. In addition, many market participants turned their attention to the HaNoi Plas 2026 Plastics and Rubber Industry Exhibition taking place in Hanoi, so transactions were limited. The price gap between suppliers was also quite large; among them, some sellers were noted offering US-origin PE at the lowest price segment of the market.

For example, with HDPE, one distributor quoted HDPE blow of Sinopec (China) for retail customers in small quantities at 35,600 VND/kg with VAT in the north; however, some buyers said the settled transaction price for China-origin HDPE blow is currently only about 34,500–35,000 VND/kg with VAT, with buyers bidding even lower. Qamar’s HDPE Film F1 still holds an offer price around 36,000 VND/kg with VAT, with buyers pressing hard on prices in the belief that prices will fall further in the coming days.

For LDPE Film, the offer price level fell sharply to 39,000–41,000 VND/kg with VAT, i.e. down 1,000–4,000 VND/kg compared to the previous week (depending on product, origin and region). For example, in the south, some US- and Korean-origin LDPE Film was offered down to 39,000 VND/kg with VAT; even LDPE 2426K of PTT (Thailand) was offered down to 40,000–40,500 VND/kg with VAT. In the north, offers for Middle Eastern-origin LDPE Film such as 4024WN of Sabic, 4025AS/1925AS of Tasnee or FT6230 of Borouge also fluctuated around 40,000–41,000 VND/kg with VAT...

For LLDPE, in the north, some traders lowered the offer price of LLDPE Film 218WJ of Sabic and FD21HS of Qamar to 31,500–32,000 VND/kg with VAT depending on quantity, a sharp drop of 1,000–1,500 VND/kg compared to late last week. Chinese-origin LLDPE Film has mostly been offered below 31,000 VND/kg with VAT, competing strongly with Sibur (Russia) goods. In the south, some large distributors offered LLDPE 218WJ of Sabic down to 31,000 VND/kg with VAT, 500 VND/kg lower than the north...

summary: SE Asia PVC fell 25 USD to 700 USD/ton CFR; China down 5
Weak demand, falling ethylene and freight pressure offers
Coal SG-5 20,500 (North); AGC Vina PM66R 20,900 (South) VND/kg
paras: According to Platts pricing, the price level of PVC in China fell slightly, by 5 USD/ton compared to the previous week; however, the price in Southeast Asia fell 25 USD/ton, down to 700 USD/ton/CFR.

PVC resin prices in Asia fell slightly during the week due to pressure from weak demand, downward market sentiment and falling ethylene input material prices. According to market participants, the Southeast Asian import market is under pressure from lower offers for China-origin goods, medium-level demand and a downward trend in freight rates. Meanwhile, in South Asia, India extended the tax exemption period for some petroleum and imported chemical goods, including PVC, from June 30 to July 15, helping to keep the market relatively stable.

In our country, after the drop at the start of this week, PVC sellers are holding prices cautiously. Currently, coal-based PVC SG-5 of Chinese origin still holds the lowest price at 20,500 VND/kg with VAT in the north. Although this is the lowest price in the market, the gap with oil-based goods has narrowed to only about 500–1,000 VND/kg, so it is not particularly attractive to buyers either.

Especially in the south, oil-based PVC PM66R from domestic manufacturer AGC Vina held the lowest price at 20,900 VND/kg with VAT, creating very strong competitive pressure. PVC SE950/SE1000 of Shintech (US) origin was noted with one large distributor offering it at 21,500 VND/kg with VAT, notably lower than the general price level of the previous week...
